role_delineation_check: The Social Worker manages the clinical training schedule and coordinates the HHA referral, while the Patient Navigator identifies caregiver anxiety via sentiment checks, audits demographic accuracy in Atlantis, and flags the training gap to ensure the transition does not pivot to LTC.
narrative_summary: Mrs. Evelyn Reed, an 83-year-old recovering from post-ICU pneumonia, reached clinical stability but remained dependent on supplemental oxygen and nebulizer treatments. Her daughter, Sarah, felt a profound sense of panic regarding her ability to manage the respiratory equipment at home, as the facility had not yet provided hands-on instruction. The Patient Navigator acted as a supportive liaison by documenting this training gap in Atlantis and alerting the Social Worker to the family's hesitation, ultimately securing a bedside teaching session that restored the family's confidence in the home discharge plan.
demographic_audit_note: Audited the facility face sheet against the Atlantis record; identified a discrepancy in the primary phone number provided for the daughter (Sarah). Updated Atlantis with the correct mobile number to ensure the HHA and MA can reach the family for the first visit.
v_card_flyer_status: Successfully inserted the Healing Partners V-Card into Sarah's smartphone and provided the program flyer. Sarah confirmed she now recognizes the number as 'Healing Partners' for future coordination calls rather than an unknown solicitor.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Conducted the 24-hour pulse call; Sarah reported a sentiment score of 8/10 following the respiratory training session. Confirmed the home is ready for the oxygen concentrator delivery and verified the time window for the first MA visit.
atlantis_final_sync: Logged the confirmed Friday discharge date in Atlantis; scheduled the Medical Assistant for the first post-discharge visit on Saturday morning following HHA portal confirmation.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Mrs. Reed completes her acute antibiotic course for pneumonia but requires 2L of continuous oxygen for exertion.
The daughter, Sarah, expresses high anxiety during a bedside chat, stating she has never operated a nebulizer and fears the oxygen tanks will run out at night.
The Social Worker schedules a bedside teaching session after the PN flags the family's reluctance to discharge without a clinical demonstration.
The Atlantis portal shows the HHA has received the referral but the status remains 'Pending' with no assigned nurse for the weekend transition.

format_2_triples.situation: Sarah is tearful and mentions potentially looking for an Assisted Living facility instead of home because of the oxygen equipment.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Verify_Sentiment_Score and flag the specific caregiver training gap to the Social Worker in Atlantis.

format_2_triples.situation: The HHA referral status has not changed in the Atlantis portal for 48 hours.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Maintain_Granular_Wait-Status_Timeline in Atlantis and ask the SW for a status update on the HHA acceptance.

format_2_triples.situation: Sarah is confused about what happens the first hour Mrs. Reed is back in her house.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educate the family on Day 1 expectations, including the arrival of the Medical Assistant and the role of the HHA nurse.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to conduct the final discharge huddle to see if the family's anxiety about the nebulizer is addressed by the floor nurse.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This deferral ensures the PN does not interfere with the facility's standard discharge protocol, though it risks a last-minute refusal of services if the anxiety persists.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Request_Joint_Family_Meeting_via_SW to discuss the transition plan, document the family's sentiment score, and ensure the SW is aware of the specific training needs before the discharge date is finalized.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This action stays within the liaison scope by using the SW as the primary coordinator while ensuring the family's barriers are formally recognized in the transition plan.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the respiratory therapy department directly to schedule Sarah's training and then call the oxygen vendor to ensure they provide a detailed instruction manual for the specific concentrator model.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: While this ensures the training happens quickly, it violates the rule against contacting facility departments and outside vendors, overstepping into clinical coordination and vendor management.
